title: [GRAY ZONE] Anzu Robotics: Technology licensed from adversary entity: DJI (Shenzhen DJI Technology Co., Ltd
detail: Technology licensed from adversary entity: DJI (Shenzhen DJI Technology Co., Ltd.)

title: [GRAY ZONE] Anzu Robotics: Teardown confirms adversary hardware: PCB layouts align exactly with DJI Mavic 3
detail: Teardown confirms adversary hardware: PCB layouts align exactly with DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise. Board part numbers match. Controller RRC01 is relabeled DJI RC Pro with identical firmware. Camera module, gimbal, propulsion all DJI-sourced. DJI P1 OcuSync chipset present — proprietary ASIC that cannot be independently sourced.

title: [GRAY ZONE] Anzu Robotics: FCC filing reveals adversary components: Anzu did NOT disclose DJI relationship
detail: FCC filing reveals adversary components: Anzu did NOT disclose DJI relationship in initial FCC filings (Dec 2023). Only disclosed after security researchers publicly revealed connection in mid-2024. House Committee specifically cited this omission.

title: [GRAY ZONE] Anzu Robotics: 50% of components from adversary nations — partial pass-through
detail: 50% of components from adversary nations — partial pass-through

title: [GRAY ZONE] Anzu Robotics: Firmware signed with adversary crypto keys: Firmware signed and encrypted with D
detail: Firmware signed with adversary crypto keys: Firmware signed and encrypted with DJI cryptographic keys — DJI retains root key control. Built on DJI SDK. SecNeo/BangCle Chinese security framework present — not confirmed decrypted. MAVProxyUser documented firmware accessible on DJI's own CDN, contradicting independent hosting claims.

title: [GRAY ZONE] Anzu Robotics: SDK from adversary manufacturer embedded: Firmware signed and encrypted with DJI
detail: SDK from adversary manufacturer embedded: Firmware signed and encrypted with DJI cryptographic keys — DJI retains root key control. Built on DJI SDK. SecNeo/BangCle Chinese security framework present — not confirmed decrypted. MAVProxyUser documented firmware accessible on DJI's own CDN, contradicting independent hosting claims.

title: [GRAY ZONE] Anzu Robotics: SDK contacts adversary endpoints: White Knight Labs pentest confirmed data goes
detail: SDK contacts adversary endpoints: White Knight Labs pentest confirmed data goes exclusively to Aloft servers — but found references to Chinese domains within system (remediated by Aloft). Texas AG alleges Aloft itself is a DJI-integrated platform. GitHub repo documented firmware accessible on DJI CDN.

detail: Teardown confirms adversary hardware: Specta Air has identical PCBs to DJI Air 3. Same DJI P1 chipset. RF emission reports differ only by date and logo removal. Firmware forked from DJI with branding stripped. No independent technology whatsoever.

title: [GRAY ZONE] SkyRover / Knowact: Firmware signed with adversary crypto keys: SkyRover app connects DIRECTLY to DJ
detail: Firmware signed with adversary crypto keys: SkyRover app connects DIRECTLY to DJI cloud infrastructure. Jon Sawyer found identical encryption keys. Users can log in using existing DJI credentials. This is the most direct DJI connection of any white-label — no Aloft-style intermediary.
